title: thymus loevyanus var. piligerus
intro: Thymus loevyanus var. piligerus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading, and woody perennial herb belonging to the mint family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Thymus loevyanus var. piligerus have been used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as skin conditions and wounds, promoting overall health and well-being. Herbal preparations of creeping thyme include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, relieve stress, and promote a restful sleep.
